title: diyaminauclea zeylanica
intro: Diyaminauclea zeylanica, also known as the "Indian gentian" or "katuk", is a plant species native to tropical Asia, particularly in the countries of India and Sri Lanka. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Diyaminauclea zeylanica include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying the leaves to make a tea or powder, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
